incomeTypes.label: Ostatní příjmy §10 (spekulace)
incomeTypes.description: Capital gains/losses from selling or swapping crypto as a non-business individual. Taxed under §10 of the Income Tax Act (zákon o daních z příjmů). Not subject to social or health insurance contributions.

incomeTypes.label: Ostatní příjmy §10 (těžba/staking)
incomeTypes.description: Income from mining, staking, and airdrops received by a non-business individual. Market value at receipt is taxable income and becomes the cost basis. Taxed under §10. Not subject to social or health insurance.

notes: The 3-year holding period exemption (§4 odst. 1 písm. ze) took effect 15 Feb 2025 under the Digital Finance Act (zákon o digitálních financích).
The 3-year time test applies retroactively: no transitional provisions exist, so assets acquired before 15 Feb 2025 also qualify if held 3+ years at the time of disposal.

Two independent exemption paths exist: (1) Time test (§4(1)(zj)) — held 3+ years, exempt up to a CZK 40,000,000 annual cap shared with securities/shares; (2) Value test (§4(1)(zk)) — total annual gross proceeds from crypto disposals ≤ CZK 100,000, exempt regardless of holding period. Neither test is currently modeled — apply manually.
The 23% rate threshold is CZK 1,676,052 for 2025 (36× average wage of CZK 46,557).
Crypto is classified as 'other income' (ostatní příjmy) under §10 of the Czech Income Tax Act (zákon o daních z příjmů č. 586/1992 Sb.).
Both FIFO and weighted arithmetic average are accepted cost basis methods. Once chosen per asset, the method must be used consistently.
Crypto-to-crypto swaps are taxable disposal events.
Income under §10 is NOT subject to social or health insurance contributions.
Losses from crypto under §10 can offset crypto gains in the same tax year but CANNOT be carried forward. They also cannot offset income from other §10 sub-categories (e.g. securities).
The basic taxpayer allowance (sleva na poplatníka) of CZK 30,840 is a TAX CREDIT, not a deduction — it reduces computed tax, not taxable income. It is NOT modeled in the brackets above.
If mining/staking/airdrops are conducted at commercial scale, they fall under §7 (business income) instead, which IS subject to social and health insurance.
Market value at the time of receipt becomes the cost basis for mining, staking, and airdrop income.
